scenario.prompt: On a high-stakes work project, you naturally gravitate toward...

options.text: Owning the written deliverables — reports, proposals, and narratives
Designing the visual presentation and data visualization
Shaping the tone and rhythm of how the message lands
Prototyping, building, and hands-on execution

scenario.prompt: When facing a complex strategic decision at work, you...

options.text: Build a structured framework — data, criteria, and weighted trade-offs
Read the stakeholders and build consensus before deciding
Spend time in deep reflection to clarify your values and priorities
Look for patterns from precedent — what has history or nature shown?

scenario.prompt: Your team needs to rethink the office workspace. You...

options.text: Immediately start sketching floor plans and visualizing the flow
Ask deeper questions: what kind of culture do we want this space to reflect?
Research and prototype using design tools or virtual floor plan software
Write a brief outlining the vision, principles, and rationale

scenario.prompt: To decompress after an intense work week, you most naturally...

options.text: Play, listen to, or create music — it resets you completely
Tackle a puzzle, a side project, or something analytically engaging
Train, hike, cook, or work with your hands — physical activity restores you
Gather with people you care about — social energy recharges you

scenario.prompt: Your ideal professional development experience would be...

options.text: A hands-on workshop, field experience, or active simulation
A solo retreat for deep reflection and personal clarity
An immersive outdoor experience connecting work to the natural world
A philosophical or values-based retreat on leadership and meaning

scenario.prompt: A colleague is visibly struggling in a meeting. You...

options.text: Catch their eye, create space for them, and check in afterward
Send a supportive message with relevant resources after the meeting
Follow up with a thoughtful written note or email
Identify what's causing the difficulty and propose a structural fix

scenario.prompt: When navigating a major career transition, you rely most on...

options.text: Deep inner work — values clarification, journaling, and extended reflection
Mapping options visually and creating structured diagrams of possibilities
Processing the transition emotionally through music and creative outlets
Physical activity and hands-on projects that keep you grounded

scenario.prompt: In your professional life, you're most drawn to work that...

options.text: Connects to natural systems — sustainability, ecology, or living environments
Centers on human connection, community, and collective well-being
Addresses fundamental questions about meaning, justice, or purpose
Leverages technology to scale impact and solve systemic problems

scenario.prompt: When evaluating whether to take a new role, your primary question is...

options.text: Does this work matter — does it connect to something meaningful?
Can I craft narratives and ideas that genuinely move people?
Does the strategy make sense — is there a clear, logical path to impact?
Can I shape how things look, feel, and function in the world?

scenario.prompt: In your ideal role, you would spend significant time...

options.text: Building systems, tools, or platforms that create scale and leverage
Creating or curating content with strong aesthetic and emotional resonance
Doing deep, focused individual work with significant autonomy
Working directly with natural environments or living systems

likert.statement: Written and verbal communication are genuine professional strengths of mine.
I can adapt my language and communication style for different audiences and contexts.
I gravitate toward roles that involve crafting narratives, explanations, or persuasive content.
Language patterns — in literature, media, law, or rhetoric — consistently engage my attention.
Quantitative analysis, systems thinking, and logical frameworks are core to how I work.
I'm drawn to roles involving strategy, data, research, or complex problem-solving.
I evaluate claims rigorously and naturally look for evidence before drawing conclusions.
I find genuine intellectual satisfaction in deconstructing complex problems into components.
Visual thinking — design, architecture, mapping, or data visualization — is a professional strength.
I'm attuned to aesthetics, spatial relationships, and how things look and feel.
In my best work, I'm translating ideas into visual or spatial form.
I navigate new environments quickly and have a strong intuitive sense of space.
Music is not a hobby — it's a fundamental part of how I process and create.
I'm deeply sensitive to rhythm, cadence, and the emotional texture of sound.
I bring a musician's ear for pattern, structure, and timing to my professional work.
Creating, performing, or analyzing music is one of my most authentic modes of expression.
Physical craft — athletic, culinary, surgical, or artisanal — is central to my professional identity.
I do my best thinking and problem-solving when physically engaged.
Hands-on execution and embodied skill are where I add the most distinctive value.
Kinesthetic learning — learning by doing — has always been my most effective mode.
Reading people, building trust, and navigating complex human dynamics are core professional skills.
I thrive in roles involving leadership, facilitation, coaching, or stakeholder management.
I have a track record of building effective teams and resolving interpersonal conflict.
Emotional intelligence is something I actively develop and apply in professional relationships.
Self-awareness — knowing my values, triggers, and growth edges — is a professional priority.
I do my best work with significant autonomy, clear personal ownership, and time to think.
I have a strong, stable sense of professional identity and the type of work that fulfills me.
Reflective practices — journaling, coaching, therapy, or meditation — are part of my professional toolkit.
Work connected to natural systems, living environments, or ecological thinking energizes me.
I notice environmental patterns and systemic relationships that others often overlook.
Sustainability, conservation, or human-nature relationships are professionally meaningful to me.
The natural world has been a consistent source of insight, restoration, and inspiration throughout my career.
Purpose and meaning are non-negotiable criteria when I evaluate professional opportunities.
I'm drawn to work at the intersection of ethics, philosophy, and human well-being.
Questions about what makes a life well-lived shape my professional choices.
I need my work to contribute to something larger than organizational metrics or personal advancement.
Technology fluency and digital systems thinking are core professional competencies of mine.
I'm drawn to roles involving software, data, automation, or digital product development.
I pick up new platforms and tools quickly and enjoy the process of mastering them.
I think carefully about how technology shapes culture, power, and human experience.
